newsletters.subject: Import AI 457: AI stuxnet; cursed Muon optimizer; and positive alignment
newsletters.reason: Covers 3 high-priority AI/LLM topics: (1) AI security — fast16 as metaphor for AI supply-chain sabotage, (2) Muon optimizer silently killing neurons + Aurora fix — direct relevance to LLM training infrastructure, (3) Positive alignment paper from Oxford/DeepMind/OpenAI/Anthropic/Stanford — core AI safety concept

linked_articles.aurora_paper: Aurora: A Leverage-Aware Optimizer for Rectangular Matrices (Tilde Research)

newsletters.subject: The Next War Is Already Here. The West Isn't Ready. — Yaroslav Azhnyuk, The Fourth Law & Guest Host Noah Smith
newsletters.reason: Primarily geopolitics and military drone autonomy. While AI-powered drone autonomy (5 levels) is discussed, the content is about warfare tactics and defense industry, not LLM/Agent technology. Marginal overlap with AI safety (dual-use AI, autonomous weapons) but not core wiki territory.

newsletters.subject: 🎙️ How I AI: HTML is the new Markdown: How Anthropic engineers are building with Claude Code
newsletters.reason: Interview with Thariq Shihipar (Anthropic Claude Code engineer) on practical coding agent workflows. Key insights: HTML replacing Markdown for AI output, compute allocation mindset, disposable micro-apps, living design systems in HTML, effective prompting patterns, 99% token usage on process not production. Directly relevant to coding agents, developer tooling, and prompt engineering.

linked_articles.code_with_claude_event: Live recording at Code with Claude event

newsletters.subject: AI Is Coming for Junior Jobs First
newsletters.reason: Labor market/societal impact of AI on junior positions. Not technical AI/LLM content. Marginal overlap with broader AI impact discourse but not core wiki territory.

newsletters.subject: The last six months in LLMs in five minutes
newsletters.reason: Comprehensive LLM landscape summary Nov 2025–May 2026 covering: model crown changes (Claude Sonnet 4.5 → GPT-5.1 → Gemini 3 → GPT-5.1 Codex Max → Claude Opus 4.5), coding agents crossing quality barrier, OpenClaw emergence, open-weight model surge (Gemma 4, GLM-5.1, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B), HTML-over-Markdown debate, Shopify's River coding agent, LLM shebang lines. Dense with wiki-relevant entities and concepts.
